Prof. M. Mustafa Çetin’s Publications Among Most Cited in JACS

We are thrilled to announce that Assistant Professor M. Mustafa Çetin’s research has been recognized by the Journal of the American Chemical Society (JACS) as some of the most cited works between 2020-2021. Prof. Çetin’s paper titled “Integration of Enzymes and Photosensitizers in a Hierarchical Mesoporous Metal–Organic Framework for Light-Driven CO2 Reduction” (DOI: 10.1021.jacs.9b12828) was specifically highlighted for its significant contribution.

In a commendation letter addressed to Prof. Çetin, Erick M. Carreira, the Editor-in-Chief of JACS, expressed gratitude for sharing such groundbreaking science with the journal’s audience. The editor further expressed enthusiasm for receiving more of Prof. Çetin’s exceptional scientific contributions in the future.

This recognition reflects the dedication and outstanding contributions of Prof. Çetin and his research team to the field of chemistry. We remain committed to pushing the boundaries of scientific knowledge and making a lasting impact on the global scientific community.
